questions.questionText: The number of optical isomers possible for [Cr(C<sub>2</sub>O<sub>4</sub>)<sub>3</sub>]<sup>3$$-$$</sup> is ____________.

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : Ethyl pent-4-yn-oate on reaction with CH3MgBr gives a 3$$^\circ$$-alcohol.

Statement II : In this reaction one mole of ethyl pent-4-yn-oate utilizes two moles of CH3MgBr.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
(B) Statement I is false but Statement II is true.
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Let M and m respectively be the maximum and minimum values of the function
f(x) = tan$$-$$1 (sin x + cos x) in $$\left[ {0,{\pi \over 2}} \right]$$, then the value of tan(M $$-$$ m) is equal to :

questions.questionText: The set of all values of K > $$-$$1, for which the equation $${(3{x^2} + 4x + 3)^2} - (k + 1)(3{x^2} + 4x + 3)(3{x^2} + 4x + 2) + k{(3{x^2} + 4x + 2)^2} = 0$$ has real roots, is :

questions.questionText: A box open from top is made from a rectangular sheet of dimension a $$\times$$ b by cutting squares each of side x from each of the four corners and folding up the flaps. If the volume of the box is maximum, then x is equal to :

questions.questionText:

x + y + z = 4,

9x + 4y + (28 + [$$\lambda$$])z = [$$\lambda$$] has a solution is :

questions.questionText:
Let Z be the set of all integers,

$$A = \{ (x,y) \in Z \times Z:{(x - 2)^2} + {y^2} \le 4\} $$

$$B = \{ (x,y) \in Z \times Z:{x^2} + {y^2} \le 4\} $$

$$C = \{ (x,y) \in Z \times Z:{(x - 2)^2} + {(y - 2)^2} \le 4\} $$

If the total number of relation from A $$\cap$$ B to A $$\cap$$ C is 2p, then the value of p is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the solution curve of the differential equation (2x $$-$$ 10y<sup>3</sup>)dy + ydx = 0, passes through the points (0, 1) and (2, $$\beta$$), then $$\beta$$ is a root of the equation :

questions.questionText: The angle between the straight lines, whose direction cosines are given by the equations 2l + 2m $$-$$ n = 0 and mn + nl + lm = 0, is :

questions.questionText: If $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to \infty } \left( {\sqrt {{x^2} - x + 1}  - ax} \right) = b$$, then the ordered pair (a, b) is :

questions.questionText: 3 $$\times$$ 7<sup>22</sup> + 2 $$\times$$ 10<sup>22</sup> $$-$$ 44 when divided by 18 leaves the remainder __________.

questions.questionText: Let z<sub>1</sub> and z<sub>2</sub> be two complex numbers such that $$\arg ({z_1} - {z_2}) = {\pi  \over 4}$$ and z<sub>1</sub>, z<sub>2</sub> satisfy the equation | z $$-$$ 3 | = Re(z). Then the imaginary part of z<sub>1</sub> + z<sub>2</sub> is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: An online exam is attempted by 50 candidates out of which 20 are boys. The average marks obtained by boys is 12 with a variance 2. The variance of marks obtained by 30 girls is also 2. The average marks of all 50 candidates is 15. If $$\mu$$ is the average marks of girls and $$\sigma$$<sup>2</sup> is the variance of marks of 50 candidates, then $$\mu$$ + $$\sigma$$<sup>2</sup> is equal to ________________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\int {{{2{e^x} + 3{e^{ - x}}} \over {4{e^x} + 7{e^{ - x}}}}dx = {1 \over {14}}(ux + v{{\log }_e}(4{e^x} + 7{e^{ - x}})) + C} $$, where C is a constant of integration, then u + v is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let S = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9}. Then the number of elements in the set T = {A $$ \subseteq $$ S : A $$\ne$$ $$\phi$$ and the sum of all the elements of A is not a multiple of 3} is _______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The probability distribution of random variable X is given by :

X 1 2 3 4 5
P(X) K 2K 2K 3K K

questions.questionText: Water drops are falling from a nozzle of a shower onto the floor, from a height of 9.8 m. The drops fall at a regular interval of time. When the first drop strikes the floor, at that instant, the third drop begins to fall. Locate the position of second drop from the floor when the first drop strikes the floor.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The height of victoria falls is 63 m. What is the difference in temperature of water at the top and at the bottom of fall?

questions.questionText: Two discs have moments of inertia I<sub>1</sub> and I<sub>2</sub> about their respective axes perpendicular to the plane and passing through the centre. They are rotating with angular speeds, $$\omega$$<sub>1</sub> and $$\omega$$<sub>2</sub> respectively and are brought into contact face to face with their axes of rotation coaxial. The loss in kinetic energy of the system in the process is given by :

questions.questionText: A monochromatic neon lamp with wavelength of 670.5 nm illuminates a photo-sensitive material which has a stopping voltage of 0.48 V. What will be the stopping voltage if the source light is changed with another source of wavelength of 474.6 nm?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A coaxial cable consists of an inner wire of radius 'a' surrounded by an outer shell of inner and outer radii 'b' and 'c' respectively. The inner wire carries an electric current i<sub>0</sub>, which is distributed uniformly across cross-sectional area. The outer shell carries an equal current in opposite direction and distributed uniformly. What will be the ratio of the magnetic field at a distance x from the axis when (i) x < a and (ii) a < x < b ?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: if the rms speed of oxygen molecules at 0$$^\circ$$C is 160 m/s, find the rms speed of hydrogen molecules at 0$$^\circ$$C.

questions.questionText: A constant magnetic field of 1T is applied in the x > 0 region. A metallic circular ring of radius 1m is moving with a constant velocity of 1 m/s along the x-axis. At t = 0s, the centre of O of the ring is at x = $$-$$1m. What will be the value of the induced emf in the ring at t = 1s? (Assume the velocity of the ring does not change.)

questions.questionText: The light waves from two coherent sources have same intensity I<sub>1</sub> = I<sub>2</sub> = I<sub>0</sub>. In interference pattern the intensity of light at minima is zero. What will be the intensity of light at maxima?

questions.questionText: Three capacitors C1 = 2$$\mu$$F, C2 = 6$$\mu$$F and C3 = 12$$\mu$$F are connected as shown in figure. Find the ratio of the charges on capacitors C1, C2 and C3 respectively :

questions.questionText: A player kicks a football with an initial speed of 25 ms<sup>$$-$$1</sup> at an angle of 45$$^\circ$$ from the ground. What are the maximum height and the time taken by the football to reach at the highest point during motion ? (Take g = 10 ms<sup>$$-$$2</sup>)

questions.questionText: Figure shows a rod AB, which is bent in a 120$$^\circ$$ circular arc of radius R. A charge ($$-$$Q) is uniformly distributed over rod AB. What is the electric field $$\overrightarrow E $$ at the centre of curvature O ?

questions.questionText: Two simple harmonic motion, are represented by the equations $${y_1} = 10\sin \left( {3\pi t + {\pi  \over 3}} \right)$$ $${y_2} = 5(\sin 3\pi t + \sqrt 3 \cos 3\pi t)$$ Ratio of amplitude of y<sub>1</sub> to y<sub>2</sub> = x : 1. The value of x is ______________.

questions.questionText: A tuning fork is vibrating at 250 Hz. The length of the shortest closed organ pipe that will resonate with the tuning fork will be __________ cm. (Take speed of sound in air as 340 ms<sup>$$-$$1</sup>)

questions.questionText: A zener diode of power rating 2W is to be used as a voltage regulator. If the zener diode has a breakdown of 10 V and it has to regulate voltage fluctuated between 6 V and 14 V, the value of Rs for safe operation should be ___________ $$\Omega$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An ac circuit has an inductor and a resistor resistance R in series, such that X<sub>L</sub> = 3R. Now, a capacitor is added in series such that X<sub>C</sub> = 2R. The ratio of new power factor with the old power factor of the circuit is $$\sqrt 5 :x$$. The value of x is ___________.

questions.questionText: The ratio of the equivalent resistance of the network (shown in figure) between the points a and b when switch is open and switch is closed is x : 8. The value of x is ___________.

questions.questionText: A bullet of 10 g, moving with velocity v, collides head-on with the stationary bob of a pendulum and recoils with velocity 100 m/s. The length of the pendulum is 0.5 m and mass of the bob is 1 kg. The minimum value of v = ____________ m/s so that the pendulum describes a circle. (Assume the string to be inextensible and g = 10 m/s2)

questions.questionText: A plane electromagnetic wave with frequency of 30 MHz travels in free space. At particular point in space and time, electric field is 6 V/m. The magnetic field at this point will be x $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$8</sup> T. The value of x is ___________.

The K<sub>p</sub> for the reaction at 27$$^\circ$$C is x $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$2</sup>. The value of x is _____________. questions.questionText: 40 g of glucose (Molar mass = 180) is mixed with 200 mL of water. The freezing point of solution is __________ K. (Nearest integer) [Given : K<sub>f</sub> = 1.86 K kg mol<sup>$$-$$1</sup>; Density of water = 1.00 g cm<sup>$$-$$3</sup>; Freezing point of water = 273.15 K]

questions.questionText: The resistance of a conductivity cell with cell constant 1.14 cm<sup>$$-$$1</sup>, containing 0.001 M KCl at 298 K is 1500 $$\Omega$$. The molar conductivity of 0.001 M KCl solution at 298 K in S cm<sup>2</sup> mol<sup>$$-$$1</sup> is ____________. (Integer answer)

questions.questionText:
The first order rate constant for the decomposition of CaCO3 at 700 K is 6.36 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$3s$$-$$1 and activation energy is 209 kJ mol$$-$$1. Its rate constant (in s$$-$$1) at 600 K is x $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6. The value of x is ___________. (Nearest integer)

[Given R = 8.31 J K$$-$$ mol$$-$$1; log 6.36 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$3 = $$-$$2.19, 10$$-$$4.79 = 1.62 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5]

questions.questionText: If 0 < x < 1 and $$y = {1 \over 2}{x^2} + {2 \over 3}{x^3} + {3 \over 4}{x^4} + ....$$, then the value of e<sup>1 + y</sup> at $$x = {1 \over 2}$$ is :

questions.questionText: The equation of the plane passing through the line of intersection of the planes $$\overrightarrow r .\left( {\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k} \right) = 1$$ and $$\overrightarrow r .\left( {2\widehat i + 3\widehat j - \widehat k} \right) + 4 = 0$$ and parallel to the x-axis is :

questions.questionText: Let S be the sum of all solutions (in radians) of the equation $${\sin ^4}\theta  + {\cos ^4}\theta  - \sin \theta \cos \theta  = 0$$ in [0, 4$$\pi$$]. Then $${{8S} \over \pi }$$ is equal to ____________.

questions.questionText: Two circles each of radius 5 units touch each other at the point (1, 2). If the equation of their common tangent is 4x + 3y = 10, and C<sub>1</sub>($$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$) and C<sub>2</sub>($$\gamma$$, $$\delta$$), C<sub>1</sub> $$\ne$$ C<sub>2</sub> are their centres, then |($$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$) ($$\gamma$$ + $$\delta$$)| is equal to ___________.

questions.questionText: Let S be the mirror image of the point Q(1, 3, 4) with respect to the plane 2x $$-$$ y + z + 3 = 0 and let R(3, 5, $$\gamma$$) be a point of this plane. Then the square of the length of the line segment SR is ___________.

questions.questionText: Let A (sec$$\theta$$, 2tan$$\theta$$) and B (sec$$\phi$$, 2tan$$\phi$$), where $$\theta$$ + $$\phi$$ = $$\pi$$/2, be two points on the hyperbola 2x<sup>2</sup> $$-$$ y<sup>2</sup> = 2.
